description: Curated fixture corpus for mindkeep eval scenarios (#15, tightened in #28). Recall queries are now mostly synonym-based: the user-facing query word is intentionally NOT verbatim in the expected fact/ADR (we test that bm25 + tag indexing still surfaces the right rows). Distractor facts (f31..f34, a11) carry surface tokens overlapping with queries but are topically off; a healthy bm25 must still rank the on-topic rows above them.

facts.content: The mindkeep storage layer uses SQLite with WAL journal mode for crash-safe writes.
FTS5 powers full-text recall in mindkeep; bm25 ranks results lower-is-better.
facts.comment: Query 'lookup'/'search' should hit this via tags even though value uses 'recall'.

facts.content: The token estimator uses the heuristic ASCII chars divided by 4 and CJK chars divided by 2.
Write-guard rejects facts whose post-redaction content exceeds the per-kind cap (default 100 tokens).
Write-guard rejects ADRs whose post-redaction content exceeds the ADR cap (default 1500 tokens).
Pinned rows surface first in show output; ordering is pin DESC then created_at DESC.
The MemoryStore.recall API returns RecallHit dataclasses with kind, id, score, snippet, tags.
Project identity is resolved via git remote, falling back to directory hash.
SecretsRedactor scrubs API keys and tokens from text before persistence.
facts.comment: Top-1 query 'secret OR redactor' targets this; value uses 'SecretsRedactor scrubs'.

facts.content: Auto flush scheduler commits the SQLite connection on a background thread.
Session budget tracks per-session token spend and suppresses output when exhausted.
ADRs auto-assign their number column as max(number) + 1 under an RLock for concurrency safety.
facts.comment: Top-1 query 'threadsafe OR rlock OR mutex' targets this; value uses 'RLock' and 'concurrency'.

facts.content: The doctor command emits OK, WARN, and FAIL checks; --json gives machine-readable output.
show --top K caps rendered rows per kind; --budget N caps total token spend across rows.
The export command produces a portable JSON snapshot; import re-hydrates a project DB.
facts.comment: Top-1 query 'snapshot OR backup' targets this; value uses 'export ... JSON snapshot'.

facts.content: Tags are stored as comma-joined TEXT in SQLite and decoded on read into a list.
Filter pipeline applies in order; each Filter.apply returns a possibly rewritten string.
Cross-project preferences live in a global preferences.db file shared across projects.
Pinned ADRs appear before unpinned ones in list_adrs and show output.
The CLI install script supports pipx and pip user installs on Windows and Unix.
mindkeep 使用 SQLite 的 FTS5 扩展 来实现 全文检索 功能。
facts.comment: CJK synonym test: query '搜索' or '查询' must hit this via tags; value uses '全文检索'.

facts.content: 写入 守卫 会在 内容 超过 令牌 上限 时拒绝 写入,除非 传入 force=True。
钉选 的事实 会优先 出现在 show 命令 的输出 中,排序 为 pin 降序。
Migration scripts upgrade the schema between mindkeep versions in place.
Recall ranks results by bm25 score; lower scores indicate better matches.
The token estimator returns at least 1 for any non-empty text input.
Write-guard also rejects pre-redaction content exceeding 2x the cap as a huge-blob guard.
Backups are not provided by mindkeep; users should snapshot the data dir externally.
facts.comment: Note: f15 is the *intended* top-1 for 'snapshot' since it ships an export feature; f28 is a related but weaker hit.

facts.content: The CLI exit code is 0 on success, 1 on user error, 2 on storage failure, 3 on validation.
Stats subcommand prints row counts and token totals across all kinds.
Users sometimes search the FAQ before filing issues against the project.
facts.comment: DISTRACTOR for 'search/lookup' queries: token 'search' present in value but topic is FAQ docs, not the recall API. Healthy bm25 should rank f02/f25/f07 above this because those rows hit on the OR-synonym set in both value and tags.

facts.content: Manual estimation of release timelines is tracked on the project board.
facts.comment: DISTRACTOR for 'estimation/estimator' queries: token 'estimation' present in value but topic is project planning, not the token estimator.

facts.content: Diagnostics output for slow tests includes timing breakdowns per case.
facts.comment: DISTRACTOR for 'diagnostics/health' queries: token 'diagnostics' present but topic is test timing, not the doctor command.

facts.content: Concurrent integration tests run via pytest-xdist on CI workers.
facts.comment: DISTRACTOR for 'thread-safe/concurrent' queries: token 'concurrent' present but topic is test parallelism, not the RLock-guarded ADR numbering. Healthy bm25 should rank f12 above this because f12 hits multiple OR terms (concurrent + concurrency + rlock + mutex via tags).

adrs.title: Use SQLite WAL for storage
adrs.decision: Adopt SQLite in WAL journal mode as the on-disk store for facts, ADRs, sessions, and preferences.
adrs.rationale: WAL gives crash-safe concurrent reads with a single writer and avoids external dependencies.

adrs.title: FTS5 for recall
adrs.decision: Use SQLite FTS5 with bm25 ranking for full-text recall over facts and ADRs.
adrs.rationale: FTS5 ships with SQLite, requires no extra runtime, and covers our recall quality bar.

adrs.title: Token estimator heuristic
adrs.decision: Estimate tokens as ASCII chars divided by 4 and CJK chars divided by 2.
adrs.rationale: Cheap, deterministic, stdlib-only, and good enough for budget enforcement without a real tokenizer.

adrs.title: Dual-threshold write guard
adrs.decision: Reject writes whose post-redaction tokens exceed the cap, or whose pre-redaction tokens exceed 2x cap.
adrs.rationale: Catches both oversized clean content and huge unstructured blobs while letting redaction reduce small inputs.

adrs.title: Pin priority ordering
adrs.decision: Order list_facts, list_adrs, and show output by pin DESC then created_at DESC then id DESC.
adrs.rationale: Pinned rows are the most important; deterministic tie-breakers make tests stable.

adrs.title: Per-project DB layout
adrs.decision: Each project gets its own SQLite file under the data dir, keyed by a project hash.
adrs.rationale: Project isolation prevents cross-contamination and keeps recall scoped.

adrs.title: Global preferences DB
adrs.decision: Store cross-project preferences in a single shared preferences.db.
adrs.rationale: Preferences should persist regardless of which project the user invokes mindkeep from.

adrs.title: Stdlib-only runtime
adrs.decision: Keep mindkeep dependencies-free at runtime; rely solely on the Python standard library.
adrs.rationale: Easier install, smaller wheel, no supply-chain surface beyond CPython itself.

adrs.title: JSON output for doctor
adrs.decision: Provide --json on doctor for machine-readable health output with summary counts of OK, WARN, FAIL.
adrs.rationale: Enables CI integration and script-driven monitoring without parsing TTY output.

adrs.title: 采用 SQLite 作为 存储 后端
adrs.decision: 选择 SQLite 与 WAL 模式 作为 mindkeep 的核心 存储 后端,用于 事实、ADR 与 会话。
adrs.rationale: SQLite 自带,无外部 依赖,且支持 崩溃 安全 的并发 读取。

adrs.title: Release planning cadence
adrs.decision: Keep release timelines tracked on the planning board; estimation is informal.
adrs.rationale: We do not need formal estimation tooling for a small project.
adrs.comment: DISTRACTOR ADR for 'estimation' queries: surface overlap with f03/a03 but topic is release planning.

recall_at_5.q: lookup OR search OR retrieval OR recall
recall_at_5.comment: Synonym query: 'lookup'/'search'/'retrieval' do not appear in expected values; only 'recall' does. Distractor f31 will match on 'search'.

recall_at_5.q: validation OR limit OR cap OR guard
recall_at_5.comment: Synonym query: 'validation' is present only via tags on the expected rows, not in their values.

recall_at_5.q: approximate OR heuristic OR estimator OR estimation
recall_at_5.comment: Synonym query: 'approximate' is in tags only. Distractors f32 and a11 will match on 'estimation'.

recall_at_5.q: priority OR ordering OR pinned OR pin
recall_at_5.comment: Synonym query: 'priority' and 'pinned' are in tags only on expected rows.
Synonym query: 'health' is in tags only. Distractor f33 matches on 'diagnostics'.
Synonym query: 'durability' is in tags only on expected rows; 'journal' appears in values.
Synonym query: 'quota' is in tags only on expected rows.

recall_at_5.q: scheduler OR background OR async OR flush
recall_at_5.comment: Synonym query: 'async' is in tags only on f10.
Synonym query: 'shared' is in tags only on expected rows.
Synonym query: 'statistics' and 'counts' are in tags only on f30.

recall_top1.comment: Synonym: f15 value uses 'portable JSON snapshot' and 're-hydrates'. f28 mentions 'snapshot' but lacks 'portable'/'rehydrate', so multi-term match on f15 wins.

recall_top1.q: threadsafe OR concurrent OR rlock OR mutex
recall_top1.comment: Synonym: 'threadsafe' and 'mutex' tag-only. Distractor f34 matches on 'concurrent'.
Synonym: 'secret' is tag-only on f09; value uses 'SecretsRedactor'.

recall_cjk.comment: CJK SYNONYM: '搜索' does NOT appear in f21's value (which uses '全文检索') — must hit via tag '搜索' on f21.
